summary: Matched a first chunk of src/melee/lb/lbrefract.c, raising the file report to 25.59% matched for a 4.98 KB file. The PR added concrete implementations for fn_80022120, lbRefract_8002219C, lbRefract_80022BB8, and lbRefract_80022BD0, adjusted math helper expressions for matching, moved local refract callback data/types out of lbrefract.h into lb/types.h and lb/forward.h, and marked many lbrefract scratch entries as OK in config/GALE01/scratches.txt. There were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Added lbrefract.c implementations for fn_80022120, lbRefract_8002219C, lbRefract_80022BB8, and lbRefract_80022BD0.
fn_80022120 computes texture/address offsets from lbRefract_CallbackData fields and optional output pointers, with null checks for each destination pointer.

lbRefract_8002219C initializes lbRefract_CallbackData fields, computes buffer size with GXGetTexBufferSize, selects callback pairs by texture format-like arg2 cases 3, 4, and 6, calculates aligned row/block sizes, and returns -1 for unsupported cases including default and case 5.
lbRefract_80022BB8 and lbRefract_80022BD0 manipulate static counter lbl_804336D0[0], with lbRefract_80022BD0 reporting and asserting on negative use count.
Changed lbrefract.c includes to include lbrefract.h directly, lb/types.h, dolphin/gx/GXTexture.h, and baselib/debug.h.
Adjusted atan2f, atanf, and lbRefract_80022DF8 expressions for matching, including explicit float casts around M_PI/M_PI_2 and using truthiness checks like if (x).
Moved RefractCallbackTypeA/B/C typedefs into src/melee/lb/forward.h and added a forward declaration for struct lbRefract_CallbackData.
Moved the callback-data struct into src/melee/lb/types.h as struct lbRefract_CallbackData.
Simplified src/melee/lb/lbrefract.h by removing broad includes, pragma region markers, local TODO type definitions, and extern declarations for callback functions.
Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt entries around lbrefract and related math functions from percentage statuses to OK, preserving scratch metadata and ordering many historical attempts.
smart_moves: Separated public declarations from shared type definitions: lbrefract.h now keeps function prototypes while forward.h/types.h carry callback types and struct definitions needed across the lb subsystem.
Used address comments on static prototypes, such as /* 021F34 */ and /* 022120 */, to keep unmatched/static helper locations searchable during decomp work.
Kept unknown callback implementations as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS where the exact signatures were not yet resolved, while giving the newly matched callback fn_80022120 a concrete signature.
Used explicit casts to float constants in math code, a common matching tactic when double promotion or constant type affects generated code.

Preserved switch/goto structure in lbRefract_8002219C, including an explicit labeled shared return block and an irregular-switch comment, rather than over-cleaning into more idiomatic C that might not match.
decomp_lessons: For library modules, move temporary local structs out of public headers once their role becomes reusable; prefer lb/types.h for concrete structs and lb/forward.h for forward declarations and callback typedefs.

Do not leave broad includes in a module header when only forward declarations are needed; this PR removed GXMisc, OSCache, cobj, memory, pobj, and tobj includes from lbrefract.h and relied on baselib/forward.h.
Matching math library-like functions can require careful control of literal types and conditions, such as casting M_PI constants to float and using if (x) instead of if (x != 0.0f).
When decomping texture/refract code, GXGetTexBufferSize and alignment masks such as 0xFFFFFFE0 and 0xFFFFFFC0 may be important evidence for texture buffer layout and row pitch.
Scratch entries should be advanced to OK when incorporated, even if many historical scratch attempts point at the same address under different names.
naming_conventions: New shared struct name uses the module-prefixed style lbRefract_CallbackData rather than the older local _RefractCallbackData/RefractCallbackData names.
Callback typedefs are named RefractCallbackTypeA, RefractCallbackTypeB, and RefractCallbackTypeC, indicating unresolved semantic roles but preserving distinct signatures.
Unidentified static local helpers use fn_800xxxxx names rather than public lbRefract_ prefixes.
Public/unscoped lbrefract symbols retain lbRefract_800xxxxx address-based names until better semantic names are known.
The header guard was changed from _lbrefract_h_ to GALE01_021CE8, matching the address/section-oriented guard style present in parts of the project.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit `(float) M_PI` and `(float) M_PI_2` casts to control floating-point constant codegen.
Changed zero comparisons from `x != 0.0f` to `x` in math functions where that better matched generated code.
Used a shared switch return label with `goto block_11` in lbRefract_8002219C, suggesting decompilation matched the compiler's branch structure more closely than a refactored switch.
Marked the switch as irregular, preserving evidence that the compiler output did not map cleanly to a simple high-level switch.
Kept static prototypes with address comments to align helper functions with original binary addresses.
Represented the global/static counter as `static int lbl_804336D0[0x10]`, leaving exact data semantics unresolved while matching storage shape.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbRefract_CallbackData fields unk0-unk5 or callback0/callback1 with semantic names.
Find later lbrefract PRs matching lbRefract_PObjLoad, lbRefract_80021CE8, lbRefract_800222A4, lbRefract_8002247C, and lbRefract_80022998.
Search for other PRs that move temporary module-local structs from headers into lb/types.h or forward.h.
Search for math matching changes involving explicit float casts for M_PI, M_PI_2, NAN, or INF.
Inspect later scratch cleanup around lbrefract::func_80022D1C, lbrefract::func_80022DBC, and func_80022E68.
